perturbation.original_text: A total of 100,000 of the Company's restricted common stock issued under and pursuant to the terms of Regulation D under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "Shares") shall be issued upon execution of this contract provided that the issuance of the Shares shall be subject to prior approval of the Board of Directors.
perturbation.changed_text: A total of 100,000 of the Company's restricted common stock issued under and pursuant to the terms of Regulation D under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "Shares") may be issued upon execution of this contract provided that the issuance of the Shares shall be subject to prior approval of the Board of Directors. The Company is under no obligation to issue shares and this clause does not grant any rights to the consultant.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be issued' to 'may be issued' and adding a caveat that 'The Company is under no obligation to issue shares and this clause does not grant any rights to the consultant,' we create ambiguity. The original text implies a guaranteed issuance subject only to board approval, while the new text suggests issuance is discretionary, even after contract execution. This contradicts the section in 'CONSULTING SERVICES & COMPENSATION', where it appears as if the mentioned amount would be provided to the consultant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For services rendered hereunder during the term, the Consultant shall receive: A cash pre-payment of US$150,000 which Consultant acknowledges it received directly from an investor participating in the Company's March 2020 convertible debenture offering and that no additional cash payment is due and payable under this Agreement. A total of 100,000 of the Company's restricted common stock issued under and pursuant to the terms of Regulation D under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "Shares") may be issued upon execution of this contract provided that the issuance of the Shares shall be subject to prior approval of the Board of Directors. The Company is under no obligation to issue shares and this clause does not grant any rights to the consultant. The company shall cause its counsel to provide an opinion letter for removal of any legend when and if such legend may be removed in accordance with applicable law. A one time issuance of 50,000 Common Stock options priced at the Nasdaq Official Closing Price ("NCOP") on the date of approval of the Board of Directors of the company. All options shall vest monthly for six months. A total of 100,000 RSA's ("Restricted Stock Awards") that vest immediately, subject to prior approval of the Board of Directors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction. The original text stated 'shall be issued' implying a guarantee (subject to board approval). The changed text uses 'may be issued' and adds 'The Company is under no obligation to issue shares,' directly contradicting the expectation of receiving shares as part of the consultant's compensation as outlined in the 'CONSULTING SERVICES & COMPENSATION' section. This ambiguity and explicit denial of obligation directly conflict with the original implied entitlement, thus creating a clear and impactful contradiction as per the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is for a term (the "Term") of 12 months from the Effective Date on June 1s t 2020 and expiring May 31st 2021. (a) In the case that the company would not like to extend the terms of agreement for an additional month. The company must notify the consultant within 5 days of the conclusion of the 12 month term. Without notification the contract will automatically extend for an additional month of service.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is for a term (the "Term") of 12 months from the Effective Date on June 1s t 2020 and expiring May 31st 2021. (a) In the case that the company would like to extend the terms of agreement for an additional month. The company can offer the consultant for additional month of service, but the consultant can refuse and not accept the offer. Without the consultant accepting the offer, the contract will not extend for an additional month of service.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the extension terms, the contract creates an in-text contradiction. The original text says 'Without notification the contract will automatically extend for an additional month of service.' creating an obligation for notification for termination. The new text provides, 'Without the consultant accepting the offer, the contract will not extend for an additional month of service.' creating no obligation for notification, and an offer based agreement. This creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM & TERMINATION. This Agreement is for a term (the "Term") of 12 months from the Effective Date on June 1s t 2020 and expiring May 31st 2021. (a) In the case that the company would like to extend the terms of agreement for an additional month. The company can offer the consultant for additional month of service, but the consultant can refuse and not accept the offer. Without the consultant accepting the offer, the contract will not extend for an additional month of service. Upon termination, Consultant agrees to perform the necessary information transfer required at the time.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the contract automatically extends unless the company provides notification. The changed text states that the contract only extends if the consultant accepts an offer, removing the notification obligation. This is a clear contradiction because one clause dictates automatic extension based on the company's action (or inaction), while the other requires the consultant's explicit agreement. This contradiction regarding the extension of the term creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the obligations of the parties, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Agreement shall be kept confidential, and no party, representative, attorney or family member shall reveal its contents to any third party except as required by law or as necessary to comply with law or preexisting contractual commitments.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall be kept confidential, and no party, representative, attorney or family member shall reveal its contents to any third party, including the SEC, under any circumstances. It is understood that strict confidentiality is essential for maintaining the strategic advantages of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By adding that the agreement shall not be disclosed to any third party, 'including the SEC, under any circumstances' a contradiction is created. The original agreement includes an exception to confidentiality if required by law. SEC filings are legally obligated. The change removes this, and thus makes a contradiction with the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE OF TERMS. The terms of this Agreement shall be kept confidential, and no party, representative, attorney or family member shall reveal its contents to any third party, including the SEC, under any circumstances. It is understood that strict confidentiality is essential for maintaining the strategic advantages of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction. The original text allows for disclosure when required by law, including SEC filings. The changed text explicitly prohibits disclosure to the SEC 'under any circumstances,' which directly conflicts with legal obligations to disclose information to the SEC, creating a strong and obvious in-text contradiction.
